page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7t.6xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most operations, with a PassMark CPU score of 28,868.06 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,900.0. It demonstrates solid capabilities in cryptographic and vector calculations, supported by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES. Memory bandwidth tests show 127.7 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 63.99 GB/sec for large block write operations. Notably, the instance achieves a strong performance tier in text generation for small LLM models at 294.62 tokens/sec, while maintaining average performance for medium models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g7t.6xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale network-attached storage independently. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate-scale language model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7t.6xlarge is a general-purpose, KVM-based virtual machine featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate solid mid-range performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 28,868.06 and a strong text generation speed of 294.62 tokens/sec on small LLM models. Lacking bundled storage, it offers qualitative cost efficiency by decoupling compute from storage scaling. It is suited for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate inference workloads.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7t.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7t.6xlarge features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96 GB RAM, delivering balanced general-purpose compute performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio for reliable enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores running at 2.7 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero bundled local storage
Strong text generation performance for small-scale language models
Suitable for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
